meta.reportLabel: Tue PM — flash PMIs OUT (EZ downturn eases, UK services contracts, US softer), oil 3-mo low / into PCE
meta.coverage: USD, EUR, GBP, JPY, AUD, NZD, CAD · 18 pairs + DXY + JPYBASKET + GER40
meta.regime: Dollar-strong / yen-weak, intact. Warsh's hawkish FOMC stuck — DXY 13-mo high ~101, dots flip next move to a HIKE. USD/JPY ~161.5 (near 1986 low); intervention risk coordination-flavoured after the Katayama-Bessent call. New wrinkle: flash PMIs show a US services / UK services growth crack sitting under the crowded dollar into PCE.
meta.nextBigEvent: US PCE — Thu Jun 25 (the marquee test of Warsh's 'broader than energy' call)

dailyRead: The Tuesday picture firmed rather than turned: the dollar-strong / yen-weak core is intact, and the flash PMIs have now printed and broadly fit the regime — with one small two-way wrinkle into PCE. The reads: Eurozone — the private-sector downturn eased and inflation pressures softened (composite still sub-50 after May's 48.5), a marginal EUR positive but not a break of the dovish-ECB frame; UK — services business activity CONTRACTED for the first time since April 2025, the cleanest confirmation of the GBP soft spot the hawkish-BoE bid had masked; US — services growth muted, optimism faltering, employment falling, a subtle softening that sits awkwardly under a dollar at a fresh 13-mo high (~101) and a very crowded long-USD book into Thursday's PCE. The JPY intervention story stayed at the escalated level — Katayama's call with US Treasury Sec Bessent reaffirmed US-Japan readiness to COORDINATE FX action 'if needed', so the next move could be JOINT, not unilateral; USD/JPY ~161.5 (near the 1986 low), the yen having surrendered all its Apr 30 gains. Oil slid a touch further: Brent ~$76.7, WTI ~$73.7 (a fresh ~3-mo low) on the 60-day Iranian oil license + 30M+ bbl through Hormuz, while the Friday Burgenstock technical talks stay cancelled (Iran tying them to a Lebanon ceasefire). Net: carry the dollar-strong / yen-weak lean, but two soft spots now sit under the crowded dollar — a muted US services PMI today and PCE Thursday — while the JPY book's intervention risk is two-sided and coordination-flavoured. Defend, don't chase.

strength.drivers: Core PCE firm; CPI 4.2% (May); claims 229k. Fed 3.50-3.75%, hawkish hold, dots flip next move to a HIKE.
strength.forward: 🦅 Regime intact: median '26 dot ~3.8%, 9/18 see a hike. DXY 13-mo high ~101. But today's US flash services PMI was muted (employment fell) — a small crack ahead of PCE Thu, the only near-term fade. Crowded long.

strength.drivers: Core sticky; CPI 2.8%. BoE 3.75%, hawkish 7-2 hold, 2 hike votes — but UK flash services CONTRACTED (first since Apr 2025).
strength.forward: 🦅→ Hawkish 7-2 BoE floor holds the rate story, but today's UK services contraction re-opens the cut-tail under the rate-bid GBP. Watch the GBP legs. Next BoE Aug.

strength.drivers: Trimmed mean ~3.9%; unemp 4.5%. RBA 4.35%, hawkish hold, minutes: 'inflation risks to the upside'.
strength.forward: ⚖️🦅 RBA floor + risk-on beta vs a bid dollar. AUD CPI Wed + jobs Thu are the local catalysts. Next RBA Aug.

strength.drivers: Core ~1.5%; PPI hot. BoJ 1.00% (hiked Jun 16); spot squeezed ~161.5 (near 1986 low).
strength.forward: 🦅↔ Destination unchanged, spot squeezed. Katayama-Bessent call signals US-Japan COORDINATION readiness → intervention risk now two-sided and joint-flavoured, the swing. Japan CPI Thu. Next BoJ late Jul.

strength.drivers: Headline easing; recovery establishing. RBNZ 2.25%; Q1 GDP firm +0.8%.
strength.forward: ⚖️ Firm GDP floors NZD; hawkish USD caps NZD/USD. Net neutral into Jul 8 RBNZ.

strength.drivers: May CPI hot + broadening: headline 3.2% (from 2.8%), CPI-ex-gas 2.2% (from 2.0%); gas +33.2%, groceries +4.3%. BoC 2.25% (parked).
strength.forward: ⚖️ Hot/broadening CPI gives CAD a floor; but oil lower again (Brent ~$76.7, WTI ~$73.7, 3-mo low) re-presses the export side. Net less heavy than Sunday. Next BoC late Jul.

strength.drivers: Core ~2.3%; unemp ~6.3%. ECB 2.25% (hiked Jun 11, dovish 'no cycle' frame). EZ flash PMI: downturn eased, inflation pressures softened (still sub-50).
strength.forward: ⚖️↘ Pure USD-leg: hawkish Fed pushes EUR/USD down, dovish ECB frame caps bounces. The easing downturn is a modest positive, not a turn. Next ECB late Jul.

catalysts.event: Flash PMIs (DE, EZ, UK, US) — OUT
catalysts.note: EZ downturn eased / inflation softened (still sub-50); UK services CONTRACTED (first since Apr 2025); US services muted, employment fell — a small US/UK growth crack into PCE.
Tests the RBA's 'inflation risks up' lean — the AUD swing.
Direct test of Warsh's 'broader than energy' call; today's muted US services PMI is the first hint a soft core is possible — the dollar's only near-term fade. Median '26 PCE 3.6%, core 3.3%.

catalysts.event: Australia jobs · Japan CPI · final US GDP
catalysts.note: AUD labour swing; JPY pipeline check.
USD/JPY ~161.5; Katayama-Bessent reaffirmed coordinated-action readiness. Biggest near-term swing for the whole JPY book, now joint-flavoured.
Clock ticking; Friday's Burgenstock technical round stays CANCELLED (Iran tying it to a Lebanon ceasefire). The residual oil-respike tail stays a notch wider.
NZD's next live event; hike case fading on oil.
All next decisions parked to late July.

geopolitics: Flows still beat diplomacy on price. Washington's 60-day Iranian oil license plus 30M+ barrels shipped through Hormuz last week pushed Brent to ~$76.7 and WTI to ~$73.7 (a fresh ~3-month low). The diplomatic track stays stalled: Friday's Burgenstock technical talks remain cancelled, with Iran reportedly demanding Israel halt the re-erupted Lebanon fighting. The 60-day MOU clock keeps running, so the base case (truce holds, oil drifts lower) is intact — but the slipped first technical round plus the Lebanon flare keep the oil-respike tail a notch wider. Transmission: oil → energy/headline inflation → CB hawkishness → FX. Base case keeps the dollar bid, supports GER40 and risk-beta AUD/NZD, and does NOT buy a Fed cut (Warsh marked inflation up into the oil drop); for CAD the broadening CPI partly offsets the renewed oil drag. Tail (re-widened a notch): an MOU stall / Lebanon escalation re-spikes oil (rescues CAD, hurts AUD/NZD/GER40, hands USD & JPY competing haven bids) — the persistent ~$4,195 gold bid is the thin premium for exactly this.
